mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with immediate positive market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with INDPRO changes with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels indicate persistent inflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 60 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 315.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50 percent of observed instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real gross domestic product growth surpasses 2.5 percent annually, it confirms strong aggregate demand that exceeds baseline corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5 percent is followed by negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.
